_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, scorecard-refine-local cron)
_meta.note: Jacksonville, FL — Mayor Deegan (D) + Council President Carrico (R) + 3 council members (Carlucci, Howland, White, all R). Evidence-based finalization for the LOCAL tier. Rosters verified current June 2026. Confidence = evidence_local. Every TRUE/FALSE cites a primary or reputable source; null where no evidence (no party-default inferences). Adds the v5.0 local categories (public_justice, refuse_state_overreach) missing from prior records.

set.office: Mayor of Jacksonville (sitting · filed for 2027 re-election Apr 16, 2026)

profile.background: 9th Mayor of Jacksonville (sworn July 1, 2023); former WTLV-TV First Coast News anchor; founder, The Donna Foundation / 26.2 with Donna marathon; three-time breast cancer survivor. FSU 1984. Filed for re-election April 16, 2026 (March 2027 primary).

donna-deegan.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting Mayor of Jacksonville (sworn July 1, 2023). Filed for re-election April 16, 2026; primary March 9, 2027. Catholic (Bishop Kenny HS alumna). HARD evidence (cited): publicly pro-choice (CNN Lead appearance May 2024 opposing FL's 6-week ban; endorsed in 2023 by EMILY's List and Ruth's List, both abortion-rights PACs) — sanctity_of_life q0 FALSE. First Jax mayor to issue an LGBTQ+ Pride Month proclamation (2024), flew Pride flag over City Hall, signed EO creating Mayor's LGBTQ+ Advisory Board, marched as grand marshal in River City Pride — biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 FALSE. Publicly opposed Gov. DeSantis's property-tax constitutional amendment as Tallahassee 'taking control away from local governments'; the 2023 mayoral race was nationally framed as a rebuke of the DeSantis playbook (refuse_state_overreach q0/q3 TRUE — affirmative local-control / subsidiarity posture). Vetoed City Council immigration bill (Ord. 2025-0138) blocking city funds from serving undocumented immigrants ('political theater'); let an earlier immigration-jail ordinance take effect WITHOUT her signature; Hispanic Outreach Director flagged ICE locations on Instagram and Deegan reinstated her saying it was 'not illegal'; FL AG James Uthmeier issued a criminal subpoena to her office Feb 2026 alleging coordination to impede federal immigration enforcement — border_immigration q1/q2 FALSE. City building gun registry (logbook of CCW holders' names/firearms entering city buildings) operated under her administration July 2023–2024; FL AG sued Jacksonville for $5M in May 2026 alleging knowing maintenance of an illegal registry; she attended White House Office of Gun Violence Prevention launch Sept 2023 — self_defense q0/q1 FALSE. FY26 budget funded JSO at $638M plus $387M JFRD with $100.1M for first-responder salary/pension increases — robust budgetary support of LE, but per the FXBG rubric high-bar this is routine governance, not the affirmative public 'Back the Blue' advocacy public_justice q0 requires, so that cell is left null. Economic stewardship q2 (balanced-budget orientation): mixed evidence — FY26 held millage flat with zero reserves drawdown and stated intent to pivot to pay-as-you-go, but FY25 drew $47.2M from reserves — null pending clearer pattern. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on Christian liberty, election integrity, family/schools (city does not run schools), or specific public-justice sub-questions — those cells null, not penalized.

0.note: Publicly opposed FL's 6-week abortion ban on CNN (May 2024); endorsed by EMILY's List and Ruth's List, both abortion-rights PACs that vet for pro-choice stance.
First Jacksonville mayor to issue an LGBTQ+ Pride Month proclamation (2024); flew Pride flag over City Hall.

2.note: Signed Executive Order creating an 11-member Mayor's LGBTQ+ Advisory Board (2024).

4.note: Affirmatively promotes LGBTQ+ identity in city policy: Pride proclamation, advisory board EO, grand-marshal Pride march.

0.note: FY26 budget funds JSO at $638M plus JFRD $387M and $100.1M first-responder raises — robust budgetary support of LE but routine governance, not affirmative public advocacy required by this question.
Publicly opposed DeSantis property-tax constitutional amendment as Tallahassee 'taking control away from local governments,' projecting $300M+ Jacksonville impact.

3.note: Affirmative subsidiarity language framing local control vs. state-level intrusion as a values stand.

1.note: Vetoed Ord. 2025-0138 blocking city funds from supporting undocumented immigrants; FL AG criminal subpoena Feb 2026 alleging coordination to impede federal immigration enforcement.

2.note: Vetoed enforcement-cooperation ordinance; let earlier immigration-jail ordinance take effect WITHOUT her signature — actively opposes federal preemption against sanctuary-adjacent policies.

0.note: City building gun registry (logbook of CCW holders' names/firearms) operated under her administration July 2023–2024; FL AG sued Jacksonville for $5M in May 2026 alleging knowing maintenance of an illegal registry.

1.note: City maintained a de facto firearm registry under her administration; supportive of additional gun controls (attended White House Office of Gun Violence Prevention launch Sept 2023).

set.office: City Council President (Council District 4 · sitting; term to July 1, 2027)

profile.religion: Impact Church, Jacksonville (per official city bio)
profile.background: District 4 (Southside) since Nov 2020 special election; re-elected unopposed 2023; elected 57th Council President 17-1 on May 27, 2025 (sworn July 1, 2025). VP of Strategic Initiatives, Boys & Girls Clubs of Northeast Florida; MPA University of North Florida. Stated three core values: 'faith, family, and future.'

kevin-carrico.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: District 4 (Southside), Council President 2025-26 (term to July 1, 2027). Impact Church (per his official city bio page). HARD evidence (cited): voted YES on Councilman Diamond's FY2025-26 budget amendments blocking city taxpayer funds from going to abortion-related, DEI, and immigration-related uses, stating 'These are values that I value and I wasn't going to vote against them' — sanctity_of_life q0 TRUE. Publicly states 'faith' as one of his three governing values; inauguration included a Mayor's Inaugural Prayer Breakfast (christian_liberty q0 TRUE — affirms right to publicly profess faith in office). Led the 1/8-mill property tax rate cut passed 10-9 on Sept 23, 2025 (first property-tax-rate cut in years, ~$13.4M/yr taxpayer relief); forced Finance Committee to cut $13M+ from Mayor Deegan's budget. Primary sponsor of the Jacksonville Illegal Immigration Enforcement Act (Ord. 2025-0147), passed 12-5 in spring 2025, mandating city-agency cooperation with federal/state immigration enforcement and funding JSO mobile-fingerprint readers — border_immigration q1/q2 TRUE. Affirmative pro-LE advocate: pushed JSO funding to 100% of Sheriff Waters's request, sponsored 2025-0147 to give JSO tools for immigration enforcement, publicly stated 'public safety remains my top priority' — public_justice q0 TRUE. INTEGRITY CAVEAT: active State Attorney subpoena re: alleged interference in JEA board appointments; conflict-of-interest scrutiny over Boys & Girls Clubs city grants. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on biblical_marriage votes (Jacksonville HRO predates his term; no Pride proclamation roll call), family/schools (City Council does not run Duval schools), election integrity, refuse-state-overreach (he generally aligns with state, not against it), or 2A — those cells null.

0.note: Voted YES on Diamond FY2025-26 budget amendment blocking city taxpayer funds from going to abortion-related services; 'These are values that I value and I wasn't going to vote against them.'
Publicly affirms 'faith, family, and future' as his three core governing values; presidency inauguration included a Mayor's Inaugural Prayer Breakfast.

Affirmative pro-LE advocate: pushed JSO funding to 100% of Sheriff's request, primary sponsor of Ord. 2025-0147 funding JSO mobile-fingerprint readers, publicly states 'public safety remains my top priority.'

1.note: Primary sponsor of the Jacksonville Illegal Immigration Enforcement Act (Ord. 2025-0147), mandating city-agency cooperation with federal/state immigration enforcement; 'Jacksonville is setting the standard for cities across America.'

2.note: Sponsored / voted for ordinances explicitly aligning Jacksonville with federal/state immigration enforcement and blocking city support for undocumented immigrants — opposes sanctuary-city posture, supports federal preemption.

profile.background: State Farm insurance agent (Hendricks Ave) since 1981. Fifth term overall (1987-1994, 1999-2003, 2019-present); current At-Large Group 4 term to July 1, 2027 (term-limited). Council President 2001. Appointed by Gov. Rick Scott to FL Commission on Ethics; chaired Commission 2016-2017. Self-described moderate Republican.

matt-carlucci.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Group 4, current term ends July 1, 2027 (TERM-LIMITED — Matt McCullough has entered race to replace him). Self-described moderate Republican; State Farm agent. HARD evidence (cited): publicly opposed Councilman Diamond's FY2025-26 budget amendment banning city funding for 'abortion and abortion-related services,' calling it a 'poison pill' of 'divisive rhetoric' and saying on the floor 'Have you no damn shame?' — sanctity_of_life q0 FALSE. Co-sponsored Ord. 2020-0244 with Aaron Bowman and LeAnna Cumber restoring LGBT anti-discrimination protections to Jacksonville's Human Rights Ordinance (passed 15-4 in June 2020) — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE (affirmatively expanded LGBT identity in city public policy). Voted NO on the FY2025-26 one-eighth-mill property tax rate cut (10-9 passage), saying 'You can't cut your way to a greater city. I don't see waste' — local fiscal-restraint posture is opposed; left economic_stewardship q2 null (the question is narrowly about deficit spending / BBA, not millage votes). Voted NO on Ord. 2025-0138 (immigration-funding restrictions) and NO on the related FY2026 budget amendment banning city funds for 'illegal aliens,' arguing 'If they're here and they're not documented, that's for the feds to take care of … they're still human beings' — border_immigration q1/q2 FALSE. Has led council push to remove Confederate monuments (separate from rubric mapping). NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a cited church home, public statements on schools/CRT/SOGI/parental rights, invocation policy, election integrity, public-justice affirmative-advocacy, refuse-state-overreach, or 2A — those cells null.

0.note: Publicly opposed Diamond's FY2025-26 budget amendment banning city funds for abortion-related services; called it a 'poison pill' and 'divisive rhetoric' and asked Diamond on the floor 'Have you no damn shame?'

4.note: Co-sponsored Ord. 2020-0244 restoring LGBT anti-discrimination protections to Jacksonville's Human Rights Ordinance; passed 15-4.

1.note: Voted NO on Ord. 2025-0138 (city-grant immigration restrictions) and NO on related FY2026 budget amendment; said 'If they're here and they're not documented, that's for the feds to take care of.'

2.note: Opposed city ordinance restricting funds for undocumented immigrants; declines to layer state/federal preemption at city level.

set.office: City Council Vice President (At-Large Group 3 · sworn in as Council President June 25, 2026)

profile.background: B.A. Duke (1995), M.B.A. Chicago Booth, U.S. Navy officer 1995-1999. Executive in defense, security, and marine-safety industries. Executive Director, The Fire Watch (Florida veteran-suicide-prevention nonprofit). First won At-Large Group 3 in Feb 2022 special election (succeeded late Tommy Hazouri); re-elected to full term to July 1, 2027. Council Vice President 2025-26; voted in 5/26/2026 as Council President for 2026-27 year (sworn 6/25/2026). Filed for 2027 AL-3 re-election; unopposed as of May 2026 with $320K raised.

nick-howland.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Group 3, sitting Council VP (sworn in as Council President June 25, 2026 for 2026-27 year). Filed for 2027 re-election to AL-3, unopposed as of May 2026 ($320K+ raised); Charter §5.05 two-term limit not yet triggered (2022 partial term typically doesn't count). HARD evidence (cited): As Finance Vice Chair (2025-26), voted YES on the FY2025-26 one-eighth-mill property tax rate cut (passed 10-9 Sept 23, 2025), publicly stating '$1 in the hands of an individual is better than $1 in the hands of the government' — economic_stewardship q2 TRUE (affirmative fiscal-restraint posture). Sheriff's Office Council Liaison for three years; received Jacksonville Sheriff's endorsement in his 2022 special-election win; publicly states 'public safety is my top priority' — public_justice q0 TRUE. Public floor advocate for Ord. 2025-0147 (Jacksonville Illegal Immigration Enforcement Act, passed 12-5 in spring 2025), on the record: Jacksonville is 'standing with our president, with our governor, with our state legislature to secure our borders' — border_immigration q1/q2 TRUE. As Rules Committee Chair (May 2025), Howland was the first councilmember to publicly expose Jacksonville's covert City Hall gun registry (private security logging armed visitors' names and firearm types in apparent violation of FL §790.335 since July 2023); his disclosure led to a $5M state AG suit against the city and a DeSantis 'accountability' demand — self_defense q1 TRUE (opposes firearm registries; concrete affirmative-2A action). On Diamond's FY2025-26 abortion-defunding amendment, urged Diamond procedurally to withdraw and bring as standalone legislation (no substantive pro-life statement of his own) — sanctity_of_life left null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on biblical_marriage, family/schools (no city-level rubric-relevant vote), christian_liberty (no cited church or invocation statement), election integrity, refuse-state-overreach, or self_defense q0 — those cells null. Religion / church: NO EVIDENCE FOUND.

2.note: As Finance Vice Chair, voted YES on the 1/8-mill property tax rate cut (10-9 passage Sept 23, 2025); publicly stated '$1 in the hands of an individual is better than $1 in the hands of the government.'

0.note: Sheriff's Office Council Liaison three years; Jacksonville Sheriff endorsement in 2022 special election; states publicly 'public safety is my top priority.'

1.note: Public floor advocate for Ord. 2025-0147 (Jacksonville Illegal Immigration Enforcement Act, passed 12-5); 'standing with our president, with our governor, with our state legislature to secure our borders.'

2.note: Backed first-in-nation local criminalization of illegal presence and city cooperation with federal/state enforcement — opposes sanctuary posture, supports federal preemption.

1.note: As Rules Committee Chair, the first councilmember to publicly expose Jacksonville's covert City Hall gun registry (apparent FL §790.335 violation since July 2023); led to $5M state AG suit and DeSantis 'accountability' demand.

set.office: City Council Member, District 12 (sitting · running for Duval County Tax Collector 2027)

profile.religion: Rise Church, Jacksonville (westside) — per official city bio
profile.background: Retired Jacksonville Fire and Rescue Department (JFRD) Deputy Director / Assistant Fire Chief — 32-year career, retired 2011; former president, Jacksonville Association of Firefighters (2002-2003). NOT a former JSO officer (correcting prior placeholder). District 12 since 2018 special election; re-elected 2019; re-elected 2023 with 65%. Council President 2024-25 (sworn July 1, 2024 19-0). Current term ends July 1, 2027. Launched campaign for Duval County Tax Collector April 15, 2025 (open seat — Overton term-limited); Overton has endorsed.

randy-white.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: District 12, sitting, current term to July 1, 2027. Background CORRECTED: retired JFRD (32-year fire career, Deputy Director / Asst Fire Chief), NOT a JSO officer. Rise Church (westside Jacksonville) per official city bio. Lame-duck on Council — launched April 15, 2025 campaign for Duval County Tax Collector (open seat, Overton-endorsed); raised ~$150K by June 2025. HARD evidence (cited): voted NO on Jacksonville's 2020 HRO LGBT-anti-discrimination expansion (Ord. 2020-0244), one of only 4 NO votes against 15 YES — biblical_marriage q4 TRUE (affirmatively opposed expansion of LGBT identity protections in city public policy). Voted AGAINST the FY2025-26 one-eighth-mill property tax rate cut (10-9 passage), proposing instead to set aside $13.4M from the revenue drop specifically for the Sheriff's Office and Fire Rescue — fiscal stewardship q2 left null (he opposed the tax cut to keep revenue, mixed signal on deficit-restraint orientation). His $13.4M public-safety carve-out for JSO+JFRD is affirmative LE/first-responder advocacy beyond routine budget — public_justice q0 TRUE. NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a directly-cited White roll-call on Ord. 2025-0138 (immigration grant restrictions) or 2025-0147 (Illegal Immigration Enforcement Act) in available reporting — per the no-inference rule, border_immigration cells left null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, schools/CRT/SOGI, invocation specifics, election integrity, refuse-state-overreach, or 2A — those cells null. Notable separately: only two council members backed a 2022 public referendum on Confederate monuments; voted against renaming Confederate Street in 2024 (not directly rubric-mapped).

4.note: Voted NO on Ord. 2020-0244 (Jacksonville HRO LGBT anti-discrimination expansion); passed 15-4, White one of only 4 NO votes.

0.note: Proposed setting aside $13.4M specifically for the Sheriff's Office and Fire Rescue to backfill the FY2025-26 millage-cut revenue drop; affirmative pro-LE/first-responder advocacy beyond routine budget.
